name: Zhong Da 79 Containerized EMALS Drone Catapult
aka: BIT electromagnetic drone launch track, container drone catapult
dateBasis: Reported aboard Zhong Da 79 / BIT video surfaced
summary: Electromagnetic (EMALS-style) aircraft-launch track assembled from individually transportable container-sized sections that connect into a runway for fixed-wing combat drones; installed on the Zhong Da 79 container ship in place of some missile modules.
notes: Launch infrastructure, not a munition; may be considered part of the existing Chinese container-ship battery entry. Flag for scope.
source1_title: Chinese Cargo Ship Converted To Launch Combat Drones - TWZ
source2_title: Chinese Merchant Ship Sports Electromagnetic Drone Launcher - USNI

name: DAMODA Drone Swarm Container
aka: DAMODA drone light-show-in-a-box, container swarm launcher
summary: A shipping container housing 12+ telescoping racks (~54 quadcopters each, ~648 drones) that rail out at the push of a button with auto-return recharge; civilian light-show product widely flagged for dual-use military swarm-launch potential.
notes: Currently civilian; weaponization is potential, not demonstrated.
source1_title: China's Drone Light Show In A Box Swarm Launcher - TWZ

name: Russian Containerized Naval Air-Defense Module Concept
dateBasis: US Army FMSO/OE Watch analysis of Russian containerized-navy concept
summary: Reported Russian experimentation with placing air-defense (Redut/Pantsir-M), EW, anti-ship and reconnaissance systems into 40-ft containers for rapid fitting onto naval and commercial vessels, part of a broader containerized-navy concept alongside Club-K.
notes: Single analytical source lineage (US Army FMSO); concept-level, not independently confirmed by Russian primary sources.
source1_title: Russian Navy Weaponizes Shipping Containers - FMSO

name: Joint Laser Weapon System (JLWS)
aka: JLWS, containerized Golden Dome laser, ex-Valkyrie IFPC-HEL
builder: US Army + US Navy joint program
summary: Planned containerized, scalable 150kW (targeting 300-500kW) high-energy laser mounted on trucks, ships or anywhere a 20-ft container can sit, for cruise-missile defense under the Golden Dome initiative.
notes: Pre-prototype; no hardware built or demonstrated yet.
source1_title: What we know about the Joint Laser Weapon System - Defense News
source2_title: US Army Redirects Laser Defense to Navy Program - Army Recognition

name: IAI Barak MX Naval Containerized Module
aka: Barak-ER container launcher, containerized Barak-ER VLS
dateBasis: Reported containerized Barak-ER fit for Netherlands MFSS
summary: Eight-cell Barak vertical-launch modules built into standard ISO containers that bolt to a vessel's aft deck to expand its magazine; selected for the Royal Netherlands Navy's Multifunction Support Ships.
notes: May overlap the existing Netherlands MSS entry; this row is the IAI launcher product specifically.
source1_title: Netherlands firms up plans for multifunction support vessels - Naval News
source2_title: New Dutch Navy Support Vessels - TWZ

name: BrahMos Containerised Launcher
summary: Concept and battery configurations placing the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile in ISO-container-disguised launch canisters for covert coastal or merchant-vessel deployment, analogous to Club-K.
notes: Container-disguise concept widely reported; fielded coastal BrahMos uses road-mobile TELs, so pure-ISO status is uncertain.

name: LIG Nex1 Haeseong Containerized Anti-Ship Module
summary: Container-sized coastal-defence launch modules for the Haeseong (SSM-700K) anti-ship missile, packaged for transportable emplacement and deck mounting.
notes: Container-module packaging plausible; ISO form factor not firmly confirmed.

name: Type 12 Containerised Launcher Concept
aka: Type 12 SSM container launcher, JGSDF island-defence container missile
summary: Reported Japanese concepts to deploy the upgraded Type 12 surface-to-ship missile from ISO-container-disguised launchers for dispersed island defence and shipboard use.
notes: Reported concept; fielded Type 12 uses road-mobile TELs.

name: MBDA Sea Venom Land-Strike Containerized Concept
aka: Sea Venom/ANL modular missile family, MBDA containerized coastal battery concept
summary: A conceptual land-strike variant of the Sea Venom/ANL anti-ship missile using a new launch canister and rail-launch modification, aimed at containerized launchers for a mobile coastal-battery / chokepoint-defence capability.
notes: Early concept/prototyping stage; no firm production timeline.
source1_title: MBDA's Sea Venom evolves family of weapons - Naval News
source2_title: DSEI 2025: MBDA outlines new weapons - ESD

name: Hanwha/Leidos Global Fast Sealift
aka: GFS, Hanwha Ocean-Leidos Global Fast Sealift arsenal ship
summary: A dual-use 217m, 31,000-ton sealift ship concept externally resembling a civilian container/vehicle carrier (~800 containers) but able to embark containerized Mk 70 PDS VLS modules, PrSM launchers and drone-mothership packages for a wartime arsenal-ship role.
notes: Concept design only; the containerized Mk 70 modules referenced are a pre-existing US system.
source1_title: Hanwha Ocean Debuts Dual-Use Vessel - Naval News
source2_title: Hanwha Ocean and Leidos partner on US Navy ship design - The Defense Post

name: Royal Danish Navy StanFlex
summary: Standardised steel weapon/sensor containers (~3 x 3.5 x 2.5m) housing complete fits - 76mm gun, Harpoon, ESSM/Sea Sparrow, torpedo, mine-laying - that slot into ship deck bays and swap between vessels; the direct precursor to later ISO-container naval systems.
notes: Modules are smaller than a 20-ft ISO container; include as the historical precursor to SH Defence's Cube. Flag for scope.

name: US Navy Containerized Delivery System (Liberator)
aka: Liberator, CDS Liberator, containerized Mk 48 torpedo launcher
builder: US Navy PEO Unmanned and Small Combatants
summary: A US Navy development effort for a containerised launcher to fire Mk 48 ADCAP heavyweight torpedoes (or similar) from unmanned surface vessels and small combatants as a modular deck payload.
notes: From ChatGPT deep-research set. Budget-disclosed program; launcher design and status not public.
source1_title: US Navy Pairs Heavyweight Torpedo with USV - Naval News
